acceptance_criteria.description: Agent catalog entries can declare private handler asset paths, and declared paths are validated to exist and stay within the agent source directory before install.
library.py use installs an agent's declared handler assets alongside its prompt file for every requested harness target: claude_code, codex, and opencode.

Reinstalling an agent is idempotent for handler assets: install_agent clears the per-harness <agent-name>-handlers/ directory before copying the currently-declared handler set, so handlers removed or renamed between installs (or dropped entirely) no longer linger on disk. Addresses Codex REGRESSION at scripts/lib/installers/agent.py:245.
A clean-install smoke test proves the full mechanism using a local fixture with no external dependency.

commits.message: test(CL-ii7a): red - cover agent handler assets
commits.purpose: Red tests and local fixtures for handler validation, copying, schema declaration, and smoke coverage.

commits.message: feat(CL-ii7a): green - install agent handler assets
commits.purpose: Installer and schema implementation that makes the red tests pass.

tests.result: RED: 4 failed for the expected reasons; installs still succeeded with missing/traversal handlers, dry-run lacked vendor_handler operations, and no handler files were copied.
RED: 1 failed and 1 passed; schema did not yet declare the handlers property.
RED: prompts installed but all three handler files were missing.

tests.command: XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/xdg-data-cl-ii7a-tests UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache-cl-ii7a uv run --no-sync pytest tests/test_library_py_installers.py tests/test_validate_library.py -q

tests.result: PASS: library.yaml is valid against docs/schema/library.schema.json.

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache-cl-ii7a uv run --no-sync python -m py_compile scripts/lib/installers/agent.py scripts/validate-library.py scripts/library.py tests/test_library_py_installers.py tests/test_validate_library.py
git stash push -- scripts/lib/installers/agent.py && uv run pytest tests/test_library_py_installers.py -k test_reinstall_agent_removes_stale_handler_assets -q; git stash pop
tests.result: RED: 1 failed. Without the fix, the previously-installed stale-handler.sh survived the second install (assert not stale_handler.exists() failed). Fix restored via git stash pop.
GREEN: 1 passed. After the fix, the stale handler is removed on reinstall while the kept handler remains.

scope_changes.description: No implementation-time scope expansion. Work stayed in library/meta and used only local fixtures.

downstream_impacts: Agent catalog entries may now declare optional handlers as relative asset paths.
Agent installs copy declared handler files or directories into each harness agent directory under <agent-name>-handlers/.
Agent dry-runs include planned handler writes in operations and target_paths.
Agent lockfile bridge_symlinks now records installed handler target to cache-path mappings.
